搁.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 阁 — the hand sets something down and leaves it there.

word.en: to put down, to set aside

sentence.en: He set the book down on the table.

搂.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 娄 — arms reach around to hug and hold close.

sentence.en: Mom sleeps with her arms around the child.

搅.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 觉 — the hand stirs things around, mixing them up.

sentence.en: She stirs the soup with a spoon.

搌.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 展 — the hand gently dabs to wipe something away.

sentence.en: She dabbed the water dry with a cloth.

搏.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 尃 — hands grapple in a fight.

sentence.en: He fought with the bad guy.

搓.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 差 — two palms rub back and forth.

sentence.en: It was cold, so he rubbed his hands.

搔.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 蚤 (flea) — the hand scratches an itchy bite.

搜.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 叟 — hands search all over to look for something.

sentence.en: The police are searching for things.

搞.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 高 — hands busy at work, doing or making something.

word.en: to do well, to manage well

sentence.en: We should do our studies well.

搡.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 桑 — the hands give a hard push.

搪.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 唐 — the hand blocks or wards something off.

word.en: to stall, to give a vague answer

sentence.en: He brushed it off with a few words.

携.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 隽 — the hand carries something or leads someone along.

sentence.en: Please carry your luggage with you.

搽.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 茶 — the hand smears or rubs cream onto skin.

摁.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 恩 — a finger presses down firmly.

摄.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 聂 — to take in or capture, as a camera does light.

摆.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 罢 — hands arrange and lay things out on display.

摊.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 难 — hands spread things out flat and open.

sentence.en: He set up a stall by the road.

摔.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 率 — losing your grip, you trip and fall down.

摞.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 累 — hands stack things up in a pile.

sentence.en: There is a stack of books on the table.

摸.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 莫 — the hand gently touches and feels something.

摽.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 票 — the hand signals or marks something.

sentence.en: The two quietly competed with each other.

撂.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 畧 — the hand drops or puts something aside.

sentence.en: He dropped his words and left.

撅.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 厥 — the hand snaps something, or sticks it out.

撇.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 敝 — to toss aside; also the name of a left-falling stroke.

sentence.en: This character has a left-falling stroke.

撑.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 掌 — the hand props something up to hold it steady.

sentence.en: It rained, so he put up his umbrella.

撕.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 斯 — both hands rip paper apart.

sentence.en: He tore the paper in two.

撤.origin: The hand radical 扌 with 育 and 攵 — to pull back and take something away.

sentence.en: The waiter took the plates away.

撩.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 尞 — the hand lifts or pushes up a hanging thing.

sentence.en: She lifted the curtain to look outside.

撬.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 毳 — to pry a lid off with a tool.

播.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 番 — the hand scatters seeds, spreading them wide.

sentence.en: The farmer sows seeds in the field.

撮.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 最 — fingers pinch up a tiny bit of something.

sentence.en: Add a pinch of salt to the soup.

撰.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 巽 — the hand writes and composes a piece.

撵.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 辇 — to drive someone away or chase them off.

word.en: to drive away, to chase off

撸.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 鲁 — the hand strokes down along something.

撼.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 感 — to shake something so hard it moves the heart.

擀.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 幹 — the hands roll dough flat with a rod.

擂.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 雷 — the hand pounds or grinds with force.

擅.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 亶 — to take something into your own hands alone.

操.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 喿 — hands take hold and manage or drill something.

sentence.en: The children play on the sports field.

擎.origin: 敬 over 手 (hand) — the hand lifts something high with respect.

word.en: to lift up, to hold up

擒.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 禽 (bird/beast) — the hand catches and captures.

擤.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 鼻 (nose) — using the hand to blow your nose.

擦.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 察 — the hand rubs to wipe and clean.

sentence.en: I wipe the table every day.

攀.origin: 樊 over 手 (hand) — hands grab and pull to climb up high.

攉.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 霍 — the hand waves and beckons or shovels.

攒.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 赞 — hands gather things together and save them up.

sentence.en: He saves money to buy books.

攥.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 纂 — the hand closes into a tight fist, gripping hard.

攮.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 囊 — the hand thrusts hard to push or stab.

支.origin: A hand 又 holding up a branch 十 — to support and hold things up.

攻.origin: 工 plus the tap radical 攵 — to strike at and attack.
